网络安全公司 SentinelOne 的研究人员在一份新报告中重构了最近对伊朗火车系统的网络攻击，发现了一个新的威胁行为者——他们将其命名为“MeteorExpresss”——以及一个前所未见的雨刷器。 7 月 9 日，当地新闻媒体开始报道针对伊朗火车系统的网络攻击，黑客通过要求乘客拨打伊朗最高领袖哈梅内伊办公室的电话号码“64411”来破坏火车站的显示屏。火车服务中断，仅一天后，黑客就关闭了伊朗交通部的网站。据路透社报道，在道路和城市发展部的计算机遭到攻击后，该部的门户网站和子门户网站瘫痪。在他的检查中，SentinelOne 首席威胁分析师 Juan Andres Guerrero-Saade 解释说，这次攻击背后的人称这种前所未见的雨刷器为“Meteor”，并在过去三年中开发了它。 Guerrero-Saade 说：“目前，我们无法将此活动与先前确定的威胁组或其他攻击联系起来，”并补充说，由于安全研究员 Anton Cherepanov 和伊朗防病毒软件，他们能够重建攻击公司。 “尽管缺乏具体的妥协指标，我们还是能够恢复帖子中描述的大部分攻击组件以及他们遗漏的其他组件。在这个关于停止的火车和流氓巨魔的古怪故事背后，我们发现了一个不熟悉的人的指纹攻击者。” Guerrero-Saade 表示，对 Padvish 安全研究人员的早期分析是 SentinelOne 重建的关键，以及“恢复的攻击者工件，其中包括更长的组件名称列表”。
“攻击者滥用组策略来分发 cab 文件来进行攻击。整个工具包由批处理文件的组合组成，这些批处理文件编排了从 RAR 档案中删除的不同组件，”Guerrero-Saade 解释说。 “使用攻击者提供的 Rar.exe 副本和密码 'hackemall' 解压档案。擦除器组件按功能拆分：Meteor 基于加密配置加密文件系统，nti.exe 破坏 MBR，以及 mssetup。 exe 锁定系统。” SentinelOne 发现，大部分攻击是“通过一组嵌套在其各自组件旁边并在连续执行中链接在一起的批处理文件进行策划的”。据报道，批处理文件通过伊朗铁路网络内网络共享中的 CAB 文件复制初始组件。从那里，批处理文件使用自己的 WinRAR 副本从三个使用 Pokemon 主题密码的附加档案中解压缩附加组件，“hackemall”在攻击期间在其他地方也被引用。 “在这一点上，执行开始分叉到其他脚本中。第一个是 'cache.bat'，它专注于清除障碍并使用 Powershell 为后续元素做好准备，”Guerrero-Saade 说。 “'cache.bat'执行三个主要功能。首先，它会将受感染设备与网络断开连接。然后它会检查机器上是否安装了卡巴斯基防病毒软件，在这种情况下它会退出。最后，'cache.bat'。 bat' 将为其所有组件创建 Windows Defender 排除项，有效地为成功感染扫清障碍，而不会遇到任何障碍。”该报告解释说，这个特定的脚本在重建攻击链方面具有指导意义，因为它包含一个攻击组件列表，为研究人员提供了要搜索的特定内容。
部署了两个批处理文件，使机器无法启动并清理事件日志。在一系列其他操作之后，update.bat 将调用“msrun.bat”，它传递“Meteorwiper 可执行文件作为参数”。 Guerrero-Saade 解释说，另一个批处理文件——msrun.bat——移动到一个屏幕锁和 Meteor 雨刷器的加密配置中。计划任务由名为“mstask”的脚本创建，然后设置为在午夜前五分钟执行流星雨刷。 “整个工具包存在一种奇怪的碎片化程度。批处理文件产生其他批处理文件，不同的 rar 存档包含混合的可执行文件，甚至预期的操作也分为三个有效负载：流星擦除文件系统，mssetup.exe 将用户锁定，并且 nti.exe 可能会破坏 MBR，”Guerrero-Saade 写道。 “这个错综复杂的攻击链的主要有效载荷是一个位于‘env.exe’或‘msapp.exe’下的可执行文件。在内部，编码人员将其称为‘Meteor’。而这个特定的 Meteor 实例遭受了严重的 OPSEC 故障（包含可能用于内部测试的详细调试字符串），它是一个具有广泛功能集的外部可配置擦除器。”根据该报告，Meteor 雨刷器带有一个参数，即加密的 JSON 配置文件“msconf.conf”。 Meteor 在从加密配置移动时擦除文件删除卷影副本并将机器从域中取出以使修复复杂化。报告称，这些只是触及了 Meteor 功能的皮毛。尽管未用于对伊朗火车站的攻击，但擦除器能够更改所有用户的密码、禁用屏幕保护程序、根据目标进程列表终止进程、安装屏幕锁、禁用恢复模式、更改启动策略错误处理、创建计划任务、注销本地会话、删除卷影副本、更改锁定屏幕图像和执行需求。
Guerrero-Saade 指出，wiper 的开发人员为wiper 创造了多种方法来完成这些任务中的每一个“但是，操作员显然在编译带有大量用于内部测试的调试字符串的二进制文件时犯了一个重大错误。后者是这表明尽管开发人员在他们的武器库中有任何先进的实践，但他们缺乏确保此类错误不会发生的强大部署管道。此外，请注意，此示例是在部署前六个月编译的，并且没有发现错误，“报告发现。 “其次，该代码是自定义代码的奇异混合体，其中包含开源组件 (cpp-httplib v0.2) 和几乎古老的滥用软件（FSProLabs 的 Lock My PC 4）。虽然这可能表明 Meteor 雨刷器是内置的是一次性的，或用于单个操作，这与外部可配置设计并列，允许对不同操作进行有效重用。”当 SentinelOne 研究人员深入研究 Meteor 时，他们发现冗余证明了擦除器是由添加不同组件的多个开发人员创建的。该报告补充说，“擦除器的外部可配置特性”表明它不是为这个特定操作而创建的。他们还没有在野外看到流星雨刷的任何其他攻击或变种。研究人员无法将这次攻击归因于特定的威胁行为者，但解释说，攻击者是“中级玩家，其不同的操作组件从笨拙和简陋到精巧而发达。” “一方面，我们有一个新的外部可配置擦除器，其中充满了有趣的功能，涉及成熟的开发过程，以及实现目标的冗余手段。甚至他们的批处理脚本也包括广泛的错误检查，这是部署脚本很少遇到的功能.他们的攻击旨在削弱受害者的系统，无法通过域管理或恢复卷影副本进行简单的补救，”格雷罗-萨德写道。
“另一方面，我们看到一个对手还没有处理他们的部署管道，使用他们的恶意软件样本，其中包含与此特定操作无关的广泛调试功能和刻录功能。” Guerrero-Saade 继续说 SentinelOne “尚无法在迷雾中辨认出这个对手的形状”，并推测它是“一个肆无忌惮的雇佣军团体”或具有各种动机的国家支持的演员。尽管他们无法确定此次袭击的原因，但他们指出，攻击者似乎熟悉伊朗铁路系统的一般设置和目标使用的 Veeam 备份，这意味着威胁行为者在发动攻击之前在该系统中度过了一段时间。据路透社报道，在袭击发生时，伊朗官员没有确认是否有赎金要求或他们认为袭击的幕后黑手是谁。 《以色列时报》指出，在 2010 年臭名昭著的 Stuxnet 攻击之后，伊朗切断了其大部分基础设施与互联网的连接。
title: 研究人员详细介绍了一个新的威胁行为者使用一种名为 Meteor 的可重复使用的雨刷器对伊朗火车系统和交通部进行的前所未见的攻击
title_en: Researchers detail a never-before-seen attack against Iran's train system and transport ministry from a new threat actor using a reusable wiper called Meteor

note: Robinhood 开盘价为 38 美元，下跌约 10%，随后回升，但在首日交易时仍下跌 8% 至 34.90 美元。据彭博社报道，在 51 家公司筹集的资金达到 Robinhood 或更多的公司中，这是最糟糕的第一天。对于 Robinhood 向散户投资者提供大部分 IPO 的实验来说，这也是个坏消息。 Robinhood 的股票定价在每股 38 美元至 42 美元的目标价格区间的底部，可能是因为担心波动性，或者可能是因为 Robinhood 想要确保其散户投资者的人气。据《纽约时报》报道，尽管 Robinhood 曾表示将最多 35% 的股份分配给零售，但实际上只有 20% 用于零售，这“表明兴趣低于预期”。 Pitchbook 分析师罗伯特·勒 (Robert Le) 表示，由于股价下跌，机构投资者——那些传统上购买 IPO 并购买了大部分 IPO 的机构投资者——如果有很多散户投资者参与，可能会对未来的 IPO 持怀疑态度。这可能意味着银行家和其他公司将避免向散户投资者提供尽可能多的未来 IPO 机会。从长远来看，糟糕的第一天交易对 Robinhood 来说可能意义不大。我的意思是，Facebook 在 2012 年进行了灾难性的 IPO——定价为 38 美元，第一天什么也没做，然后在接下来的交易日下跌。顺便说一下，Facebook 目前的股价是 358.32 美元。可以说是成功的！所以我不知道今天对 Robinhood 公司意味着什么。此外，Robinhood 向其客户出售股票——如果他们喜欢该产品，他们可能是非常忠诚的投资者。范德比尔特大学金融学助理教授乔什·怀特 (Josh White) 曾任美国证券交易委员会 (Securities and Exchange Commission) 的金融经济学家，他说，现在，散户投资者可能并不试图最大化他们的回报。他们可能只是在赌博。 “投资者正在努力最大化他们的幸福，而不是他们的财富，”怀特说。 “很多人从交易股票中获得了实用性——乐趣或快乐。”也许，他建议，这比去赌场或在 DraftKings 上赌博要好，因为公司可以用这笔钱投资于就业和增长。 Robinhood 是 meme 股票和零售投资兴起的代名词。事实上，美国证券交易委员会今天在针对尼古拉创始人特雷弗·米尔顿 (Trevor Milton) 提起的诉讼中称，米尔顿“针对他称为‘罗宾汉投资者’的一类投资者展开了一场无情的公关闪电战。”投诉继续说，米尔顿追踪了进入尼古拉股票的新 Robinhood 投资者的数量，作为成功的标志。米尔顿被指控犯有证券欺诈罪； Nikola 去年的价格在 65 美元以上达到顶峰，现在价值 12.26 美元。如果这些散户投资者损失了很多钱，那可能会给经纪人带来麻烦——而不仅仅是 Robinhood。 “最终，这些将成为所有经纪公司都想吸引的客户，”怀特说。 “如果他们现在亏了很多钱，他们会完全退出投资吗？”
note_en:
Robinhood started trading at $38, dropped about 10 percent, recovered, but still closed down 8 percent at $34.90 on its first day of trading. It’s the worst first day among 51 firms that raised as much as Robinhood or more,  according to  Bloomberg. It’s also bad news for Robinhood’s experiment at offering retail investors a chunk of the IPO.
 Robinhood’s shares priced at the bottom of  the targeted price range of $38 to $42 a share, perhaps because of concerns about volatility or perhaps because Robinhood wanted to ensure a pop for its retail investors. And though Robinhood had said it would allocate up to 35 percent of its shares to retail, only 20 percent actually went to retail, which “indicated less interest than expected,”  according to  The New York Times .
  Because the stock traded down, institutional investors — those who traditionally buy IPOs and who bought the majority of this one — may be leery of future IPOs if there are a lot of retail investors involved, according to Robert Le, an analyst for Pitchbook. That may mean bankers and other companies will avoid giving retail investors as much access to future IPOs.
 A bad first day of trading may not mean much for Robinhood in the long run. I mean, Facebook had a disastrous IPO in 2012 — pricing at $38, doing not much of anything its first day, and then falling during the following trading days. Facebook’s current share price, by the way, is $358.32. It is arguably successful! So I don’t know what today means for Robinhood the company. Besides, Robinhood sold shares to its customers — and they may be very loyal investors if they like the product.
 Now, it may be that retail investors aren’t trying to maximize their returns,  says Josh White, an assistant professor of finance at Vanderbilt University, who was previously a financial economist for the Securities and Exchange Commission. They might just be doing some  gambling. “Investors are trying to maximize their happiness, not their wealth,” White says. “A lot of people get utility — fun or joy — out of trading stock.” Maybe, he suggests, this is better than going to a casino or gambling on DraftKings because companies can use the money to invest in jobs and growth.
 Robinhood is synonymous with the rise of meme stocks and retail investing. In fact, in a complaint  the SEC filed today against Nikola founder Trevor Milton, it says that Milton “embarked on a relentless public relations blitz aimed at a class of investors he called ‘Robinhood investors.” The complaint goes on to say that Milton tracked the number of new Robinhood investors who got into Nikola’s stock as a sign of success. Milton is  being charged with securities fraud; Nikola, which peaked in price above $65 last year, is now worth $12.26.
 If those retail investors lose a lot of money, that may be trouble for brokers — not just Robinhood. “Eventually, these are going to be customers that all brokerage firms want to attract,” White says. “If they lose a lot of money now, are they going to exit investing altogether?”
title: $ HOOD 股票在交易的第一天下跌
title_en: $HOOD stock down on the first day of trading

note:
在一个大型俄罗斯模块与国际空间站对接并开始“无意中”启动其推进器后，美国宇航局和俄罗斯航天局的飞行控制员周四避免了一场灾难。当地时间上午 8 点 30 分（UTC 时间 13 点 30 分），美国宇航局任务控制中心所在地休斯顿，俄罗斯“Nauka”模块与空间站相连。之后，空间站上的俄罗斯宇航员开始准备打开通向瑙卡的舱口，但在休斯顿时间上午 11 点 34 分，瑙卡意外地开始发射其运动推进器。几分钟之内，空间站开始失去姿态控制。这是一个问题，原因有几个。首先，该站需要一定的姿态来保持与地球同步卫星的信号并与地面的任务控制中心通话。此外，太阳能电池阵列被定位为基于该预定姿态收集电力。另一个问题是空间站结构上的重力。庞大的空间站的各种组件在微重力下组装，并设计为在零重力下运行。因此，即使车辆上的小应力也会导致车站结构出现小裂缝或其他问题。由于所有这些原因，休斯顿和莫斯科的空间站飞行控制员在空间站开始漂移后迅速采取行动。上午 11 点 42 分，姿态控制完全丧失，空间站服务舱的发动机点火。随后移交给与空间站相连的俄罗斯进步车辆，该车辆开始发射推进器。这场拔河比赛抵消了 Nauka 模块推进器的活动，该活动最终在燃料供应耗尽后停止。到周四中午 12 点 29 分，姿态控制已恢复。它在地面和太空中停留了一个小时。 “哎哟！”在周四的空间站活动期间，休斯顿的首席飞行总监 Zebulon Scoville 在轮班结束后在推特上发了推文。 “那。是。一天。”到周四下午晚些时候，当美国宇航局官员召开电话会议向记者通报情况时，情况似乎已经得到控制。然而，官员们拒绝透露在空间站恢复正常状态之前任务控制中心的情况有多严重。 NASA 空间站项目经理乔尔·蒙塔尔巴诺 (Joel Montalbano) 说：“在你用完所有应急计划之前，你不会真的开始担心。” “而我们今天没有那样做。”
蒙塔尔巴诺说，美国宇航局的一个工程师团队已经在研究周四失去控制对空间站结构的影响。与此同时，俄罗斯工程师正在评估 Nauka 模块的健康状况。两组都应在周五结束前完成初步评估。对于 NASA 来说，这意味着推迟计划于周五发射的 Starliner 航天器。这项期待已久的任务是在 2019 年 12 月的首次试飞因软件问题出现问题后 19 个月来的。由于 Starliner 在第一次试飞期间无法与空间站对接，波音公司同意在载人飞行之前执行 Starliner 的第二次测试任务。美国宇航局将 Starliner 的发射时间提前至美国东部时间周二下午 1 点 20 分（世界标准时间 17 点 20 分）从佛罗里达州（UTC 时间 17 点 20 分）使用 Atlas V 火箭发射。如果飞船在下周二发射并且一切顺利，Starliner 将在下周三与空间站对接。与此同时，俄罗斯人将继续努力将 Nauka 与空间站整合起来。这是一个相当大的模块，包括船员宿舍、用于科学实验的气闸等等。它的质量约为 24 公吨，比该站最大的俄罗斯部分 Zvezda 服务模块大约 20%。 Nauka 有着悠久的历史，但迄今为止，它的历史并不光彩。由于缺乏资金和技术问题，它在 8 天前的发布被推迟了十多年。到达轨道后不久，俄罗斯在 Nauka 的主推进系统上遇到了困难，与空间站的对接被推迟。在使用备用推进器提升高度并到达空间站后，Nauka 停靠。目前尚不清楚发射后不久的推进系统问题在周四的困难中可能发挥了什么作用。
note_en:
Flight controllers at NASA and Roscosmos averted a disaster on Thursday after a large Russian module docked with the International Space Station and began to "inadvertently" fire its thrusters.
 The Russian "Nauka" module linked to the space station at 8:30 am CT (13:30 UTC), local time in Houston, where NASA's Mission Control is based. After that, Russian cosmonauts aboard the station began preparing to open the hatches leading to Nauka, but at 11:34 am Houston time, Nauka unexpectedly started to fire its movement thrusters.
 Within minutes, the space station began to lose attitude control. This was a problem for several reasons. First of all, the station requires a certain attitude to maintain signal with geostationary satellites and talk to Mission Control on the ground. Also, solar arrays are positioned to collect power based upon this predetermined attitude.
 Another concern is G forces on the station's structure. The various components of the extensive space station were assembled in microgravity and designed to operate at zero-G. So even small stresses on the vehicle can induce small cracks or other problems with the station's structure.
  For all of these reasons, space station flight controllers in Houston and Moscow acted quickly after the station started to drift. Attitude control was fully lost at 11:42 am, and engines on the space station's service module were fired. This was followed by a handover to the Russian Progress vehicle attached to the station, which began to fire its thrusters. This tug-of-war offset the Nauka module thruster activity, which eventually stopped after fuel supplies were exhausted. By 12:29 pm on Thursday, attitude control was restored. It made for quite an hour on the ground and in space. "Yeehaw!"  tweeted Zebulon Scoville, lead flight director in Houston during Thursday's space station activities, after his shift ended. "That. Was. A. Day."
    By late Thursday afternoon, when NASA officials held a teleconference to brief reporters, the situation appeared to be well in hand. Officials, however, declined to say how serious the situation got in Mission Control before the station returned to its proper attitude.
 "Until you exhaust all your contingency plans, you're not really starting to worry," said NASA's space station program manager Joel Montalbano. "And we didn't do that today."
 Already, Montalbano said, a team of NASA engineers was studying the effect of Thursday's loss of control on the station's structure. Meanwhile, Russian engineers were assessing the health of the Nauka module. Both groups should complete preliminary assessments by the end of the day Friday.
 For NASA, this meant delaying the launch of a Starliner spacecraft planned for Friday. This long-awaited mission comes 19 months after an initial test flight in December 2019 went awry due to software problems. Because Starliner was not able to dock with the space station during this first test flight, Boeing agreed to fly a second test mission of Starliner before a crewed flight.
 NASA moved the Starliner launch to no-earlier-than Tuesday at 1:20 pm EDT from Florida (17:20 UTC) on its Atlas V rocket. If the spacecraft launches next Tuesday and all goes well, Starliner will dock with the space station next Wednesday.
 Meanwhile, the Russians will continue to work toward integrating Nauka with the space station. This is a sizable module that includes crew quarters, an airlock for scientific experiments, and much more. With a mass of about 24 metric tons, it is roughly 20 percent larger than the biggest Russian segment of the station, the Zvezda service module.
 Nauka has had a long and, so far, inglorious history. Its launch eight days ago came after more than a decade of delays due to lack of funds and technical problems. Shortly after reaching orbit, Russia had difficulties with Nauka's main propulsion system, and docking with the space station was delayed. After using backup thrusters to raise its altitude and reach the station, Nauka docked. It's not clear what role the propulsion system issues experienced shortly after the launch may have played in Thursday's difficulties.
title: 俄罗斯模块与空间站对接后突然发射推进器
title_en: Russian module suddenly fires thrusters after docking with space station

note:
华盛顿特区（美国有线电视新闻网）周四在国际空间站出现了一种不寻常且具有潜在危险的情况，因为新对接的俄罗斯 Nauka 模块无意中发射了其推进器，导致与空间站的“拔河”并短暂地将其推离了位置，根据美国宇航局飞行控制员的说法。 Nauka——俄罗斯航天局上周发射到国际空间站的一个长期延迟的实验室模块——在周四早上与国际空间站对接后无意中发射了它的推进器。美国宇航局官员宣布这是“航天器紧急情况”，因为空间站失去了近一个小时的姿态（国际空间站应该保持定向的角度）控制，并且地面控制员失去了与目前在国际空间站上的七名宇航员的通信在磨难期间持续了 11 分钟。 NASA 和俄罗斯航天局 Roscosmos 之间的联合调查正在进行中。美国宇航局国际空间站计划负责人乔尔·蒙塔尔巴诺坚称，宇航员从未处于危险之中，他们也没有注意到国际空间站有任何损坏。然而，位于德克萨斯州休斯顿的 NASA 任务控制中心曾一度要求他们向空间站的窗户外面看，看是否能发现空间站的任何碎片或损坏。该事件还推迟了原定于周五发射的波音 Starliner 无人驾驶试飞到该站的发射。美国宇航局表示，此举让“国际空间站团队有时间继续检查新抵达的 Roscosmos 的 Nauka 模块，并确保该站为 Starliner 的到来做好准备。”美国宇航局人类探索和运营任务理事会副局长凯西·卢德斯说：“太空飞行很艰难，当我们引入新能力时，可能会出现故障，这就是我们为这些突发事件做准备和训练的原因。”
美国宇航局官员很快淡化了事件的严重性，将其描述为“非常激动人心的时刻”和“动态事件”。蒙塔尔巴诺说：“直到您用尽所有应急计划，您才开始担心，而今天我们不在那里。”然而，蒙塔尔巴诺也承认，在空间站进入轨道的 20 年里，推进器的意外点火只发生了“三到四次”。奥巴马政府期间美国宇航局前副局长洛里·加弗 (Lori Garver) 将这一事件描述为“提醒我们，我们对俄罗斯合作伙伴能力缺乏洞察力是一种无法控制的风险。我不确定我们是否会允许我们的一个商业合作伙伴如果他们刚刚遇到我们事先听说过的这个模块的问题，就与（该）站对接。”
note_en:
Washington DC (CNN)An unusual and potentially dangerous situation unfolded Thursday at the International Space Station, as the newly-docked Russian Nauka module inadvertently fired its thrusters causing a "tug of war" with the space station and briefly pushing it out of position, according to NASA flight controllers.
 Nauka — a long-delayed laboratory module that Russian space agency Roscosmos' launched to the International Space Station last week — inadvertently fired its thrusters after docking with the International Space Station Thursday morning.
 NASA officials declared it a "spacecraft emergency" as the space station experienced a loss of attitude (the angle at which the ISS is supposed to remain oriented) control for nearly one hour, and ground controllers lost communications with the seven astronauts currently aboard the ISS for 11 minutes during the ordeal. A joint investigation between NASA and the Russian space agency Roscosmos is now ongoing.
     Joel Montalbano, the head of NASA's International Space Station Program, insists the astronauts were never in danger and that they have not noticed any damage to the ISS. NASA's Mission Control in Houston, Texas, however, at one point asked them to look outside the windows of the space station to see if they could spot any debris or damage to the station.
    The incident also delayed the launch of the Boeing Starliner uncrewed test flight to the station, which had been set to launch on Friday.
  NASA says the move allows the "International Space Station team time to continue working checkouts of the newly arrived Roscosmos' Nauka module and to ensure the station will be ready for Starliner's arrival."
 "Spaceflight is hard, and when we bring on new capabilities there can be glitches, which is why we prepare and train for these contingencies," said Kathy Lueders, associate administrator for NASA's Human Exploration and Operations Mission Directorate.
 NASA officials were quick to downplay the severity of the incident, describing it as a "pretty exciting hour" and a "dynamic event."
 "Until you exhaust all your contingency plans, that's when you start to worry and today we just weren't there," said Montalbano.
   Yet Montalbano also acknowledged that accidental firings of thrusters have only taken place "maybe three or four times" during the 20 years that the space station has been in orbit.
 Lori Garver, the former deputy administrator of NASA during the Obama administration, described the incident as "a reminder that our lack of insight into our Russian partners capabilities is an uncontrolled risk. I'm not sure we would have allowed one of our commercial partners to dock with (the) station if they had just experienced the problems we heard about with this module in advance."
title: 新俄罗斯模块失火后国际空间站短暂失去控制
title_en: ISS Briefly Loses Control After New Russian Module Misfires

note:
自由党政府提出了一个庞大的新系统，旨在确保非法内容，如儿童色情和仇恨言论，不会出现在 Facebook、YouTube、Pornhub 和 Twitter 等日常互联网平台上。这项范围广泛的提案于周四发布并发布到网上，以便加拿大人可以在 9 月底之前提供反馈。该提案将要求政府设立一个“加拿大数字安全专员”，负责监督联邦政府提出的一系列新规则，旨在保留五种类型的非法内容——儿童色情、恐怖主义内容、煽动暴力、仇恨言论以及未经同意在网上分享私密图片。平台可能会因不遵守规定而受到严厉处罚，并且该提案为警方和加拿大情报机构打开了新的大门，在被认为涉及国家安全的情况下，他们可以卷入在线进行潜在非法活动的事件。 “我们向加拿大人展示的东西反映了我们认为最好的前进方式，”遗产部长史蒂文吉尔博特告诉《星报》。 “我们要求平台以迄今为止他们尚未采取的方式承担他们的责任，所以我认为对于绝大多数加拿大人来说，我们提出的建议很有意义。”但由于提案的规模和范围，周四只能给出第一印象的专家表示，他们有一些保留意见。
一些人说，例如，通过一个独立委员会提出的上诉程序可能会因感到被 Facebook 轻视的用户的投诉而陷入困境。其他人指出，该委员会将对言论自由问题做出判断，这一举措需要仔细分析并采取适当的保护措施。根据新规则，平台将被要求将用户标记的非法内容从其网站上移除，并拥有强大的监控系统。平台需要一定程度的透明度，以确保它们采取相应的行动。虽然提案中包含了流行的社交媒体网站，但该规定不包括通过 Rogers、Telus、Bell、WhatsApp 或 Facebook Messenger 等公司进行的私人通信。该提案仅涵盖在线通信服务提供商 (OCSP)，这是一个新的法律定义，其中包括 Instagram、Twitter 和 TikTok，但不包括其他社交媒体平台，如优步、TripAdvisor 或 MyFitnessPal。所涵盖的平台必须在 24 小时内删除非法内容并遵守其他规定，否则它们可能面临高达 1000 万美元或平台全球总收入 3% 的经济处罚——以较高者为准。如果不遵守规定，专员可以对一家公司处以高达 2500 万美元或全球总收入 5% 的罚款，以较高者为准。在恶劣的情况下，当一个平台公然违反规则时，特别是涉及恐怖主义内容或儿童色情内容时，专员可能会在法庭上寻求封锁令，这可能会使加拿大人无法访问该网站。
与新专员一起的将是一个咨询委员会和一个“加拿大数字追索委员会”，该委员会将监督一个法庭系统，在该系统中，平台用户可以在用尽平台本身的上诉后寻求对决定的追索权。追索委员会将对内容删除做出具有约束力的决定。 Guilbeault 表示，根据他与提出类似监管框架的欧洲同行的对话，他“有信心”，即追索委员会不会因社交媒体用户的投诉而陷入困境。然而，“我们希望这个监管系统能够随着时间的推移而发展，因为显然，社会习惯在变化，技术在变化，平台也在变化，”他说。加拿大反仇恨网络执行董事埃文·巴尔戈德 (Evan Balgord) 表示，根据他的经验，Facebook 经常拒绝删除仇恨内容的请求。 “从来没有听说过上诉有效，”他说。追索委员会意味着“你可以向另一个人投诉”，他补充说，并指出有一个过程，用户提交他们的故事，然后 Facebook，例如，提交他们的。 “在受害者必须自我代表的情况下，存在很大的权力失衡，”巴尔戈德说。 “如果平台——即使他们在实际做正确的事情上明显更好，并且实际上尝试做正确的事情，我们知道他们没有，那么这个在线法庭仍然会有很多案件在审理成为超额认购的问题。”
渥太华大学法学教授 Vivek Krishnamurthy 表示，他对追索权委员会感到担忧，因为它“感觉非常像一个将要对言论的合法性做出判断的法院。” “我们希望确保有强有力的程序保障，并且做出的决定……符合法治原则。”最后，他说，该提案“在主流技术平台方面不会真正改变太多。” Krishnamurthy 说：“在某些方面，我们应该将其视为非法内容法案，而不是网络伤害法案。” “您知道，许多类型的在线危害完全无法通过此解决。例如，虚假信息。”他还提到了 Parler 和 Gab 的案例，这是极右翼崇拜的两个类似 Twitter 的平台，“基本上不删除任何内容是一种自豪。”他补充说，如果规则范围内的公司在加拿大没有服务器或办事处，那么还有一个管辖权问题。 “他们正在处理非常小的问题子集，”他说。 “我真的很怀疑这是否会对任何在网上面临各种问题的人产生有意义的影响。”根据新规则，平台还必须保留潜在的非法内容和涉及国家安全的内容。他们不能删除可以合法获得的内容或识别信息。
同时，有两个提议的规则可以让平台通知警察或加拿大安全情报局（CSIS）。如果内容表明存在迫在眉睫的伤害风险，则可以通知执法部门，如果内容涉及国家安全问题，则可以通知 CSIS。政府还在考虑改变 CSIS 的运作方式，并允许情报机构通过类似于生产订单的方式从平台获取订户信息。赋予 CSIS 的新权力还可以允许该机构调查网络恐怖主义内容以外的事件，并扩展到外国干涉和间谍活动。 Guilbeault 说，他们的提议只是提供他们在物理世界中拥有的执法工具，并允许它们在数字世界中使用。然而，他补充说，决定尚未最终确定，“这是一个真正的磋商过程。”任何人都可以阅读对话，但要做出贡献，您应该是注册 Torstar 帐户持有人。如果您还没有 Torstar 帐户，您现在可以创建一个（免费） 对话是我们读者的意见，并受行为准则的约束。星报不认可这些意见。
note_en:
The Liberal government has proposed a vast new system that would be tailored to ensure illegal content, such as child pornography and hate-speech, doesn’t end up on everyday internet platforms such as Facebook, YouTube, Pornhub and Twitter.
 The wide-ranging proposal was released Thursday and put  online so Canadians can provide their feedback for it until the end of September.
 The proposal would see the government establish a “digital safety commissioner of Canada” who would oversee a slate of new rules proposed by the federal government aimed at keeping five types of illegal content — child pornography, terrorist content, incitements to violence, hate-speech and the non-consensual sharing of intimate images — off the web.
 Severe penalties could be slapped on platforms for not complying and the proposal opens up new doors for police and Canada’s intelligence agency, in cases deemed to be of national security concern, to get involved in incidents where potential illegal activity is being carried out online.
 “What we’re presenting to Canadians reflects what we feel is the best way forward,” Heritage Minister Steven Guilbeault told the Star.
 “We’re asking platforms to take their responsibilities in ways that they haven’t so far and so I think that for the vast majority of Canadians, what we’re proposing will make a lot of sense.”
 But experts — who were only able to give first impressions Thursday due to the size and scope of the proposal — said they had some reservations.
 Some say the appeals process — through an independent council — that’s on the table could become bogged down in complaints from users who feel slighted by Facebook, for example. Others noted the council will be making judgments on free speech issues, a move that needs to be analyzed carefully and have proper safeguards put in place.
   Under the new rules, platforms would be required to keep illegal content flagged by users off their websites and have robust monitoring systems. There would be a level of transparency required of platforms to make sure they are acting accordingly.
 While popular social media websites are included in the proposal, the regulations would not cover private communications through companies such as Rogers, Telus, Bell, WhatsApp or Facebook Messenger.
 The proposal only covers Online Communication Service Providers (OCSPs), a new legal definition, which includes Instagram, Twitter and TikTok, but not other social media platforms such as Uber, TripAdvisor or MyFitnessPal.
 The platforms covered would have to remove illegal content within 24 hours and follow other regulations or they could face financial penalties of as much as $10 million or three per cent of a platform’s gross global revenue — whichever is higher.
 In cases of non-compliance, the commissioner could seek a fine for a company of as much as $25 million or five per cent of the gross global revenue, whichever is higher.
 In egregious cases, when a platform is flagrantly violating the rules when it comes to, specifically, terrorist content or child pornography, there could be a blocking order sought by the commissioner in court, which could make the website inaccessible to Canadians.
 Alongside the new commissioner would be an advisory council and a “Digital Recourse Council of Canada” which would oversee a tribunal system in which users of platforms could seek recourse on decisions after exhausting appeals with the platforms themselves. The recourse council would make binding decisions on content removal.
 Guilbeault said that he was “confident,” based on his conversations with counterparts in Europe where similar regulatory frameworks have been proposed, that the recourse council would not be bogged down by complaints from social media users.
 However, “We want this regulatory system to be one that can evolve over time, because clearly, social habits are changing, technologies are changing, platforms are changing,” he said.
 Evan Balgord, executive director of the Canadian Anti-Hate Network, said it’s been his experience that Facebook frequently denies requests for hateful content to be taken down. “Never heard of an appeal working,” he said.
 The recourse council would means there’s “another person you can go complain to,” he added, noting that there’s a process where the user submits their side of the story and then Facebook, for example, submits theirs.
 “There’s a big power imbalance there with the victim having to self-represent,” Balgord said.
  “If the platform’s — even if they got significantly better at actually doing the right thing, and actually trying to do the right thing, which we know they don’t, there’d still be so many cases going before this online tribunal that it becomes a question of it being oversubscribed.”
 Vivek Krishnamurthy, a law professor at the University of Ottawa, said he had concerns about the recourse council because it “feels very much like a court that is going to be passing judgment on the legality of speech.”
 “We want to make sure that there are strong procedural safeguards and that the decisions that are made ... conform with rule of law principles.”
 In the end, he said, the proposal is “not really going to change much when it comes to mainstream technology platforms.”
 “In some ways, we should view this as an illegal-content bill rather, than an online-harms bill,” Krishnamurthy said. “Many kinds of online harms are just completely unaddressed by this, you know. Disinformation, for example.”
  He also raised the cases of Parler and Gab, two Twitter-like platforms adored by the far-right, which “make it a point of pride not to remove any content, essentially.” There’s also a question of jurisdiction, he added, if a company under the scope of the rules doesn’t have servers or offices in Canada.
 “They’re dealing with a very small subset of the issues,” he said. “I really question if this is going to make a meaningful difference to anyone who has faced, you know, various kinds of problems online.”
 Under the new rules, platforms would also have to preserve potentially illegal content and content of national security concern. They couldn’t delete content or identifying information that can be lawfully obtained.
 Meanwhile, there are two proposed rules that could see police or the Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) notified by platforms. Law enforcement could be notified if the content suggests an imminent risk of harm and CSIS could be notified if the content presents a national security concern.
 The government is also looking at changing how CSIS operates and allowing the intelligence agency to obtain subscriber information from platforms through something akin to a production order. New powers given to CSIS could also allow the agency to investigate instances beyond just terrorist content online and extend to foreign interference and espionage.
 Guilbeault said that what they’re proposing is simply giving law enforcement tools that they have in the physical world and allowing them to be used in the digital one.
 However, he added that decisions haven’t been finalized and that “this is a genuine consultation process.”

title: 加拿大提议设立数字安全专员一职，以监督违反新的有害在线内容规则的行为并对平台进行处罚
title_en: Canada proposes the creation of a digital safety commissioner role to oversee violations of new harmful online content rules and issue penalties to platforms
